title: Daily Briefing — Mar 24
headline: Markets bounce but fear rules — don’t let a green day fool you.
body: Right, so we’re seeing a classic snapback: SPY up 1.1% at 655.38 but still well below its 20 MA (672.66) and 50 MA (681.42). Fear & Greed is at 16—extreme fear territory. VIX remains elevated at 26+ and rising, not the backdrop for heroic dip-buying.

Rotation is sharp: Energy (XLE 59.63) and Industrials (XLI 163.05) are leading, while Tech (XLK 136.95) and Financials (XLF 49.27) lag in the ‘improving’ and ‘lagging’ quadrants. Always pay attention to leading sectors—Energy, Utilities, and Staples are defensive, telling you how institutions are positioning.

Contrarian signal: Retail running for the exits, extreme fear, and some big names (AAPL, MSFT, NVDA, SPY) still trading below their daily 20 MAs. Remember, don’t predict a major reversal off a green candle in a fear regime—patience, react, don’t predict. If bonds (HYG 79.44, LQD 108.56) lose another percent, that’s your ‘if bonds freak out, everything breaks’ warning.
callout_body:
SPY: 672.66 (20 MA overhead) — risk not off until reclaimed
VIX: 23.95 (20 SMA), 26+ now — elevated fear
XLK: 138.95 (20 MA) — bears in control below
XLF: Approaching 49.27 — the canary still weak
XLE: 57.05 (20 MA support) — only sector with relative strength
action_items: Stay defensive: No long exposure until SPY, XLK, XLF reclaim 20 MA on a daily close. Less trades = more wins.
Watch for bond breakdowns: If HYG closes below 79.00 or LQD under 108.00, reduce equity exposure per macro rules.
closing_quote: Abundance mindset: If you missed the first bounce, wait for the next trade—don’t chase a falling knife in a VIX 26 market.
